method_atom: a deterministic mapping that converts an ordered sequence of scalar values into a graph by connecting any two elements whenever the straight line joining them lies above all intermediate values (a convexity/'visibility' criterion), so that the resulting network's topology encodes the sequence's correlation and scaling structure; the mapping establishes a correspondence between statistical regimes of the input (periodic, uncorrelated-random, self-similar/fractal) and graph classes (regular, random, scale-free with power-law degree distribution), letting network-theoretic connectivity measures characterize the underlying sequence.
object_atom: a time series — an ordered sequence of measured data values sampled over time, whose temporal regularity, randomness, or fractal self-similarity is the property being characterized.
brief_reason: The visibility/convexity graph-construction rule and the statistical-regime-to-graph-class correspondence are the transferable machinery, while the time-series signal is the domain-specific object; the two are largely separable, though 'time series' is itself a fairly generic object rather than a single field's system.
